narrative: In the afternoon, Chestnut-backed Chickadees called back-to-back in their characteristic chick-a-dee call, while Orange-crowned Warblers sang a soft trill. Common Ravens soared overhead, calling out with their distinctive "cronk" as they circled over Tilden's open ridgelines and oak woodland. The air was filled with the persistent chirps of Bushtits and nuthatches, blending harmoniously with the ravens' calls.
